title: The Barrow of Mordavain
description: The lich Mordavain has slept in his sealed barrow for four centuries and no treasure hunter who has entered has ever returned. You are carrying better information than the others. That may or may not be enough.
author: AI

intro.passage: You are standing at the entrance to the Barrow of Mordavain, where the earth has been shaped around a sealed stone door. The air is cold here even in summer and the silence is wrong: too complete, too deliberate. Someone has been here recently. The mud at the threshold is freshly disturbed.

choices.label: push open the door
choices.hint: The stone grinds under your palms, cold and heavier than expected.

choices.label: search for another way
choices.hint: You circle the mound, scanning for gaps in the earth.

shaft-entry.passage: A ventilation shaft cuts into the barrow mound at the north side, wide enough to pass and choked with roots. Broken handholds have been carved into the stone, not recently but not ancient either. Someone prepared this. A cold draft rises from below, smelling of dust and something sweet and wrong.

choices.label: lower yourself down
choices.hint: The carved handholds are slick but they hold your weight.

choices.label: return to the door
choices.hint: You pull yourself free of the roots and circle back.

entry-hall.passage: The stone door grinds open on counterweights and you step into a hall carved from living rock. Six alcoves line the walls, each holding a stone coffin sealed with bronze bands. At the far end, a stone altar squats beneath a carving of a robed figure with too many fingers. A dark passage leads deeper beyond.

choices.label: search the alcoves
choices.hint: The bronze bands on the nearest coffin are green with age.

choices.label: approach the altar
choices.hint: The carved fingers seem to curl as you draw closer.

choices.label: head into the passage
choices.hint: Cold air drifts from the dark opening ahead and chills your skin.

the-alcoves.passage: The stone coffins are sealed and intact. At the base of the third one you find a leather pack left by the last person to enter this place. Inside: a coil of good rope, a full lantern, and a folded note that reads only: DO NOT WAKE HIM. The handwriting is shaking.

choices.label: take the supplies and proceed
choices.hint: The lantern lights on the first try. The rope is still strong.

choices.label: check the altar instead
choices.hint: The black stone at the far end of the hall draws your eye.

the-altar.passage: The altar is carved from a single black stone. Six unlit candles stand in holders shaped like grasping hands. At the center, a small iron key rests in a shallow depression, as if placed there on purpose. The carving above watches with its too-many fingers and you feel, absurdly, that it is judging your intent.

choices.label: light the candles
choices.hint: Your match hisses and the first wick catches a blue flame.

choices.label: take the iron key
choices.hint: The key is colder than the stone it rested on.

candle-lit.passage: The candles light themselves. One moment dark, the next burning with cold blue flame. The temperature drops sharply. A ghostly figure forms in the corner, ragged and silent. You understand without being told: this is what remains of the last visitor to the barrow. It gestures toward a passage hidden in the far wall.

choices.label: follow the ghost
choices.hint: The figure drifts ahead and you feel compelled to follow.

choices.label: press on through the gallery
choices.hint: You turn away from the ghost and head for the main corridor.

bone-gallery.passage: The gallery is long and narrow, its vaulted ceiling hung with calcified roots that have worked through the stone. A carpet of bones covers the floor, not arranged in any burial pattern but scattered, as if they walked here and then stopped. Passages branch left and right. Ahead stands an iron door.

choices.label: take the left passage
choices.hint: The left tunnel curves out of sight and the air feels colder.

choices.label: try the right passage
choices.hint: The right corridor looks wider and more carefully cut than the left.

choices.label: open the iron door
choices.hint: The iron is rusted but the heavy hinges still move when pushed.

left-passage.passage: The left passage curves and narrows until you turn sideways to fit. It opens into a small chamber carved from bedrock, not part of the original barrow. At its center, a crystal the size of a fist pulses with slow violet light. The light feels wrong. Something of Mordavain is held inside it.

choices.label: enter the chamber
choices.hint: The violet glow paints the walls in slow, steady pulses.

choices.label: turn back to the gallery
choices.hint: Something about the violet light warns you away from this place.

crystal-room.passage: The crystal hangs above a black stone plinth with no visible support. The violet pulse quickens as you approach, as if aware. Whatever Mordavain stored here, it is still active. The warmth you expect from something so bright is absent. It radiates cold and a low, deep pressure against your chest.

choices.label: smash the crystal
choices.hint: Your hand tightens around the heaviest thing you carry. One good swing.

choices.label: pocket the crystal carefully
choices.hint: You wrap it in cloth and feel the cold seep through.

right-passage.passage: The right passage is wider and more finished, its walls cut smooth. Thirty feet in, the ceiling has collapsed, filling the floor with rubble. You can see faint light beyond the blockage and feel moving air. There is a space on the other side. The gap is low enough to require crawling.

choices.label: crawl through the rubble
choices.hint: Sharp stone scrapes your back as you squeeze into the gap.

choices.label: turn back to the gallery
choices.hint: The collapse looks unstable. You decide not to risk it.

collapsed-section.passage: You crawl through the gap with rubble pressing your back. On the other side, you drop into a chamber at the barrow's rear. A single shaft of pale light falls from a crack in the ceiling. A door ahead stands open. The smell of cold decay beyond it is enough to stop your breath.

choices.label: enter the sanctum
choices.hint: The open door frames nothing but darkness and that terrible smell.

choices.label: retreat and leave
choices.hint: Every instinct tells you to climb back the way you came.

inner-sanctum.passage: The sanctum is domed, its ceiling painted with constellations in silver pigment that still gleams after centuries. At the far end on a carved throne sits a figure wrapped in decayed finery: Mordavain the Lich, motionless, his skull-face bowed over a closed leather grimoire. A hoard of coins and relics covers the floor before him.

choices.label: approach the throne
choices.hint: Your footsteps scatter coins that ring against the stone floor.

choices.label: grab treasure and run
choices.hint: Gold glints at your feet. You do not need to go closer.

mordavain-dormant.passage: He is not asleep in any natural sense. His robed form does not breathe. The empty eye sockets are aimed at the grimoire in his lap with an attention that has not faded in centuries. Bone-white candles burn without wax along the walls. He is dormant. He is not gone.

choices.label: steal the grimoire
choices.hint: Your fingers hover over the leather spine. It is colder than ice.

choices.label: speak to the lich
choices.hint: His name rises in your throat before you can stop it.

steal-quietly.passage: You reach for the grimoire slowly. Your fingers close around the spine. Mordavain does not move. The book is cold and heavier than it should be. You take a step back. The coins in the hoard do not shift. Another step. The bone-white candles gutter slightly, as if something exhaled.

choices.label: run for the door
choices.hint: You clutch the grimoire to your chest and turn for the door.

choices.label: linger for more treasure
choices.hint: The gold on the floor catches the candlelight. So much of it.

awaken-him.passage: You speak his name. The effect is immediate: the empty eye sockets fill with violet light, cold and slow at first, then burning. The robed form straightens in the throne. The grimoire opens in his lap without a hand touching it. A voice like stones grinding says: you have one moment to explain yourself.

choices.label: offer a trade
choices.hint: You raise your empty hands and choose your words carefully.

choices.label: draw your weapon
choices.hint: Steel rings as you pull the blade free. It feels very small.

bargain-with-him.passage: Mordavain is ancient and beyond simple anger. He tells you he has had visitors before and each of them wanted the same thing. He can feel the shape of your desire. He offers a counter: he will let you leave alive, with one item of your choosing, if you bring him something in return.

choices.label: accept his terms
choices.hint: You nod slowly, though you do not yet know the price.

choices.label: refuse the offer
choices.hint: Something in his tone tells you this deal has no good end.

lich-destroyed.passage: The crystal shatters with a sound that fills the barrow, then silence. Deep below, you hear a long fading wail like wind through a closing door. The barrow shudders once and settles. When you climb out into ordinary sunlight, the cold is gone. Something has ended that has been wrong for a very long time.

ending.title: The Long Unmaking
ending.flavor: Some victories do not feel like victories until the air warms again.

great-escape.passage: You are twenty feet from the barrow entrance when you hear the slow grinding of the stone throne rotating. You do not look back. You run. The grimoire in your pack is heavier than any book should be, but it is yours, and you clear the mound at a sprint and do not stop.

ending.title: Out Before Dawn
ending.flavor: The best tomb raiders are not brave. They are fast.

bargain-struck.passage: You leave with a gold amulet of your choosing and the distinct sense that you have agreed to something without fully knowing what. Mordavain was precise about the terms and vague about his request in return. Outside, the sun is normal. You wonder how long you have before he calls it in.

ending.title: A Deal Below
ending.flavor: He got what he wanted. You got out. For now, that will have to be enough.

empty-escape.passage: You climb back out into clean air with nothing you did not bring. The barrow stands sealed and silent behind you. You went in for a legend and came out with your life, which is not nothing. Several people before you cannot say the same. You walk east without looking back.

ending.title: Wiser Than Yesterday
ending.flavor: Coming back empty-handed is only failure if you forget what you carried out.

lich-rises.passage: The violet light in his eye sockets becomes a steady blaze. The bones on the gallery floor rattle and begin to stand. Mordavain has not spoken in centuries and his first words fill every stone of the barrow. They are not words you can understand. They are not meant for the living.

ending.title: The Waking Hour
ending.flavor: You had one job. You did the other one.

servant-ending.passage: You refuse. The violet gaze holds you in place as cleanly as a pin holds a moth. Mordavain says: then you will stay. He does not raise his voice. He does not need to. The barrow door seals itself with a sound like a final sentence, and the bone-white candles burn on.

ending.title: Indefinite Employment
ending.flavor: He is very patient. He has been waiting four hundred years. He can wait for you too.
